hallucinations.description: Multiple references in the Related Work and References sections cite papers that do not exist or have fabricated/incomplete details (e.g., future-dated arXiv preprints, vague titles without venues, or non-existent works). Examples include Shao et al. (2025) FIT-Print with arXiv:2501.15509, 'Validating CNN Integrity (2023)', 'Embedding Graphs on Grassmann Manifold (2022)', and several others that cannot be verified as real publications.
hallucinations.evidence: References section items 5, 9, 11 and inline citations in sections 2.3 and 2.4 (e.g., 'Shao et al. (2025)', 'Validating the Integrity of Convolutional Neural Network Predictions Based on Zero-Knowledge Proof (2023)')

overall_assessment: The paper contains hallucinations primarily in the form of nonexistent or fabricated citations. The methodology, experimental setup, and reported results (e.g., 1.000 symmetry similarity, 0.984 AUROC) are consistent with the provided source code and execution logs, with no evidence of faked results or mathematical errors.
